summary: Large mechanical formatting-only cleanup that ran clang-format across `src`, touching 287 files with no recorded discussion or review comments. The diff excerpt shows whitespace, brace placement, line wrapping, cast spacing, function signature wrapping, comment spacing, and blank-line normalization in camera code, with similarly broad churn across fighter, item, stage, menu, library, game-mode, effect, and sysdolphin baselib files. The value of the PR was standardizing source layout so later decomp PRs inherit consistent formatting and avoid noisy style-only diffs.
what_changed: Ran clang-format over `src`, producing 2198 added and 1719 deleted lines across 287 changed files.
Rewrapped long expressions and assignments, especially in `src/melee/cm/camera.c`, including camera bounds calculations, fov calculations, vector interpolation, and pause-camera math.
Normalized empty loop formatting from single-line `while (true) {};` to a braced multi-line loop body in `Camera_80029044`.
Reformatted inline function definitions such as `get_follow_speed`, `get_delta`, and `get_slot_pad` so wrapped parameters and opening braces follow the configured style.
Normalized cast spacing examples in the excerpt, including `(u32) Camera_80029124(...)`, `(u8*) &cm_80452C68.x304`, `(s32) fn_8002F908`, and function pointer cast spacing `(s32(*)(Vec3*)) cb`.
Removed or collapsed excess blank lines in `camera.c` and standardized inline comment spacing, e.g. `cm_803BCCA0; // Force load`.
Applied the same formatting pass to headers and source files in fighter, menu, camera, item, stage, library, game-mode, effect, video, interface, player, and sysdolphin baselib areas.
smart_moves: Kept the PR explicitly scoped to a mechanical formatting pass, making the massive diff easier to interpret as style churn rather than logic work.
Merged quickly with no review discussion, suggesting the change was treated as an infrastructure/style baseline rather than a semantic patch.
Formatted the entire `src` tree in one pass, which avoids a long tail of unrelated formatting churn leaking into future matching or cleanup PRs.
Preserved existing code constructs and even comments like `/// @remarks permuter jank`, indicating this was not a rewrite of decomp-sensitive expressions.
decomp_lessons: After a repository-wide clang-format baseline, future decomp PRs should run the same formatting before review so diffs emphasize symbol, type, and matching changes rather than whitespace.
For matching-sensitive code, clang-format can alter visual layout without changing semantics; reviewers should distinguish mechanical wrapping from expression rewrites.
Large formatting-only PRs are best isolated from functional decomp changes because they invalidate surrounding line context and can complicate blame/review if mixed with logic edits.
Headers and source files should both be included in style baselines; this PR touched `.c`, `.h`, and `.static.h` files.
Existing matching notes and permuter-jank comments should be preserved through mechanical cleanup, as shown in the camera excerpt.
naming_conventions: No new symbol naming convention is evidenced; the PR preserves existing decomp-style names such as `Camera_8002B3D4`, `cm_80452C68`, `cm_803BCCA0`, and `Stage_GetCamBoundsBottomOffset`.

The changed file list reflects existing subsystem prefixes and directory naming: `ft` for fighter, `cm` for camera, `mn` for menu, `it` for item, `gr` for ground/stage, `lb` for library, `gm` for game-mode, and `ef` for effects.
Existing character/action file naming remained unchanged, such as `ftKb_Init.c`, `ftKp_SpecialS.c`, `ftFx_SpecialLw.c`, and `ftCo_DamageScrew.c`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No explicit assembly or matching tactic was discussed in the PR text or reviews.

The diff excerpt shows clang-format preserving casts, inline functions, forced-load statements, and comments used around matching-sensitive code rather than replacing them with clearer but potentially nonmatching constructs.

Formatting changes included expression wrapping and cast-spacing normalization; future matching work should verify that such style-only edits do not hide semantic modifications when reviewing large diffs.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
No reviewer-requested naming, struct, header, or matching feedback is evidenced.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that mention clang-format regressions or style conflicts after PR 2066.
Search subsequent matching PRs touching `camera.c` to see whether the formatting baseline affected permuter or matching workflows.
Check repository tooling around January 2026 for `.clang-format` or CI changes that made this formatting pass reproducible.
